title: Your PSP Is Not Just a Vendor - It Is a Risk Partner
pain_point: Merchants choose on fees first, then later face holds, reserve changes, KYC friction, and payout delays.
paycrit_angle: Paycrit helps merchants compare provider operational risk, not just headline fees.
linkedin_hook: Most merchants ask: what is the cheapest PSP? Better question: which provider supports you when risk flags appear?
post_draft: PSP selection is not a one-time procurement event. It is an operating risk decision. If your provider risk model is opaque, your cashflow becomes fragile the moment volumes spike or behavior changes. Compare reserve terms, KYC triggers, review timelines, and escalation paths before onboarding.
cta: Assess PSP risk readiness before signing.
sources:
Public payment-industry trend synthesis
Merchant risk and payout operations themes

title: Hidden Payment Fees Are Quietly Killing Margins
pain_point: Advertised rates hide effective cost from FX markups, chargebacks, refunds, and cross-border add-ons.
paycrit_angle: Paycrit translates provider pricing into effective cost so merchants can make margin-safe decisions.
linkedin_hook: Your listed PSP rate is almost never your real payment cost.
post_draft: Headline rates are easy to compare, effective rates are not. Merchants should track total payment cost by route: domestic, cross-border, refund-heavy, and high-chargeback cohorts. The gap between quoted and realized cost is often the biggest avoidable margin leak.
cta: Review effective payment cost monthly, not just sticker rates.
sources:
Public pricing transparency discussions in payments media
Merchant operations benchmark patterns

title: One PSP Is a Single Point of Failure
pain_point: Reliance on one provider can block growth, increase disruption risk, and limit payment-method expansion.
paycrit_angle: Paycrit helps merchants design resilient payment stacks with optionality by market and risk profile.
linkedin_hook: If all revenue depends on one PSP, you do not have a payment strategy - you have concentration risk.
post_draft: As merchants scale into new markets and methods, provider fit changes. A single-PSP architecture may optimize simplicity but raises outage, policy, and category-risk exposure. Even lightweight backup routing can reduce business continuity risk.
cta: Build payment redundancy before you need it.
sources:
Cross-border and multi-provider strategy analysis
Merchant resiliency and continuity patterns
